title: Podman does not work with users created by homectl/systemd-homed.
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

All podman root-less commands fail with `Error: cannot setup namespace using newuidmap: exit status 1` with a user managed by systemd-homed.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Activate systemd-homed service, and create a user account with homectl.
2. Login as the newly created user and run any podman command.


**Describe the results you received:**

Podman fails to run.

**Describe the results you expected:**

Podman should run.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

The user's home is managed by [systemd-homed](https://systemd.io/HOME_DIRECTORY/). No user information is stored in /etc/passwd, /etc/shadow and /etc/gshadow.
User records are created on the fly by systemd-homed.

**Output of `podman version`:**



```
podman version 3.4.4
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.23.1

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/user/60237/podman/podman.sock
  security:

    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: true

  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns

  swapFree: 21445206016
  swapTotal: 21474828288

  graphDriverName: overlay
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Arch Linux running on a physical computer.

comments.body: Looks like no /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid is being configured, which is pretty much required for rootless podman.

I ran into this error only with /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid configured. I deleted them just before opening this card to see what would happen if I ran without it. It did run but it printed a warning about missing subuids first.

If the /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid accounts are back in place, can you do

$ cat /etc/subuid
$ cat /etc/subgid
$ podman system migrate
$ podman unshare cat /proc/self/uid_map
@giuseppe FYI

/etc/subuid
`ananth:100000:65536
`
/etc/subgid
`ananth:100000:65536
`
podman system migrate
`Error: cannot setup namespace using newuidmap: exit status 1
`
podman unshare cat /proc/self/uid_map
`Error: cannot setup namespace using newuidmap: exit status 1`

@rhatdan

Ok, one other thing would be if the process has already dropped privs. One thought would be you are already in a user namespace or a second one would be you already dropped caps.

$  cat /proc/sef/uid_map
$ grep Cap /proc/self/status
CapInh:	0000000000000000
CapPrm:	0000000000000000
CapEff:	0000000000000000
CapBnd:	000001ffffffffff
CapAmb:	0000000000000000
another thing to check is the mount table.  It could be systemd to mount `/usr` with `nosuid`
could you share the output of `cat /proc/self/mountinfo`?

Similarly could you check if sudo works in this environment?
$ grep -i new /proc/self/status 
NoNewPrivs:	0


Root user in the userdb is marked as "nologin" - it doesn't start GNOME session and can't be logged-in via httpd!
It works like Windows where no root user exists and Admin is wheel-like.
After sudo  
podman create works ! But btrfs subvolume get-default shows /home && echo $HOME shows /root. Then everything is created under the /root/containers/ --> root subvolume. Root is homeless.
There is home.mount unit exists in the GNOME user's graph. Maybe, its status check is necessary to ensure that Podman has storage at all when used by roootless users?

I have a simular  issue. I want to test systemd-homed and create rootless containers. The issues start when I try to pull an image.

```
[fansari@bat ldap]$ docker pull alpine

0:42 for /etc/shadow): Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid: lchown /etc/shadow: invalid argument
```

@fansari I tried almost every combination: btrfs driver + fuse-mount, default mount, wtc. Nothing works. The message that I get references STAT  error. According to [Relese notes 4.13](https://btrfs.wiki.kernel.org/index.php/Changelog#btrfs-progs_v5.15_.28Nov_2021.29) <html>
<body>
<!--StartFragment-->

overlayfs | 4.15 | Overlayfs can now use btrfs as the lower filesystem.
-- | -- | --


<!--EndFragment-->
</body>
</html>
But it still doesn't work with our btrfs 5.15 or Podman is built with wrong library. Indeed, I didn't succeed to build Containers-storage CLI in my Fedora 35 WS. 
Neither log file related to SELinux contains something related to the STAT Is Container-storage too old?

I have created a new user with homectl with  fs-type=xfs. The error messages look the same.

```
Resolved "alpine" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)                                                                                                                                                         
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...                                                                                                                                                                                             
Getting image source signatures                                                                                                                                                                                                               
Copying blob sha256:59bf1c3509f33515622619af21ed55bbe26d24913cedbca106468a5fb37a50c3                                                                                                                                                          
Copying blob sha256:59bf1c3509f33515622619af21ed55bbe26d24913cedbca106468a5fb37a50c3                                                                                                                                                          
Error: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:59bf1c3509f33515622619af21ed55bbe26d24913cedbca106468a5fb37a50c3": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): potentially insufficient UIDs or GIDs available in user namespace (requested 
0:42 for /etc/shadow): Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid: lchown /etc/shadow: invalid argument      
```

I found the issue. I followed this guide:

[https://unix.stackexchange.com/questions/176593/how-to-influence-the-assignment-of-subordinate-uids-gids-when-creating-user-acco](url)

```
user=<insertusername>
uid=$(id -u $user)
sudo usermod -v $((uid+200000))-$((uid+200000+65535)) -w $((uid+200000))-$((uid+200000+65535)) $user
```
This created the missing entries in /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid.

Seems that when you don't use systemd-homed all this is done automatically.

After setting this up I was able to pull and image.

Also containers are working.

@fansari still doesnt resolve this issue for me I get the same error as you https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/12590#issuecomment-1003027364

```shell
> cat subgid
champ:260083:65536

> cat subuid
champ:260083:65536
```

What does `podman unshare cat /proc/self/uid_map` show?
Can you execute `podman system migrate` and try this again.
I re-created the home directory using LUKS storage and the error is gone. It could be related to systemd-homed using fscrypt v1 encryption policy.

@rhatdan 

Some additional info.

ArchLinux Arm, luks with btrfs FS, 2 x sub volumes @ and @home, using systemd-homed.

```
cat /etc/sub*
champ:260083:1694527157
champ:260083:1694527157
```

@fansari Why error message blames STAT system call and not create ? Did create succeed ? I looked inside the container-storage source and didn't find single line of code that communicates with systemd-homed service or checks home area status. Homed unit status is OK but I don't know if it is enough to assume that home area is useful in the current state of GUI session started by systemd-user. Rootless container that requires uid_map and regular container artifact creation are 2 different scenario but if containers-storage ignores home area the result will be the same. I afraid that homed unit execution is also incomplete.

I use Fedora 35 Silverblue but as I found with this issue and others the distribution is still not really ready for systemd-homed.
One of the issues is that these subuids and subguids are not created automatically for podman. Other issues are missing SELinux policy so you can only use it in permissive mode. Also gdm is not aware of the user so you have to create a user with "normal" home dir before you can use a user for systemd-homed. This is a little bit "offtopic" of course but it shows that the issue with podman is not the only issue where systemd-homed is not integrated well in current Linux distributions.

What SELinux AVCs are you seeing?

Have you opened issues with systemd-homed?  It should work similar to useradd when configuring the /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid files.  Or work on a newer solution to allow user namespace support the generated user.

I have created a logfile and added it to my bug report.

https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2036108
could https://github.com/systemd/systemd/issues/21952 be the cause of this issue?

The [Christopher Smart post also mentions this issue](https://blog.christophersmart.com/2021/01/31/podman-volumes-and-selinux/) 1 year ago. 
I see another issue: btrfs subvolume create works but "subvolume directory" unlabeled at all unlike any other directory in the home area. What getsubids -g pavelsosin
0: pavelsosin 100000 65536 does **100000** mean, user range starting from 100 thousands? Is it "nobody"? According to
       _Subuid authorizes a user id to map ranges of user ids from its
       namespace into child namespaces._ 
How SELinux handles nobody?

SELinux does not care about users.  Only about XAttrs on files.  If a subvolume directory is unlabled, and this gets used for container storage, then SELinux will block containers from using it.

What getsubids -g pavelsosin
0: pavelsosin 100000 65536 
I believe this means that pavelsosin has access to UIDs 100000 and then next 65534 UIDs and can assign it to a user namespace.

@rhatdan I believe that it is correct: the issue is that btrfs subvolume create command and/or library function are disconnected from SELinux and when containers storage creates container as btrfs  subvolume it has to care about labelling of subvolume "directory" and maybe, its content too. In other words, "create" works but creates something not usable due to SELinux restrictions. Another scenario can be that the SELinux library function is called but the issue with this function is hidden in the SELinux issues list.

Perfect! This issue is on and off starting from Fedora 26 [RedHat bugzilla err 1447750](https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1447750), has duplicates in some products and closed due to Fedora 26 EOL without solution.

The last comment in the bugzilla states that it was fixed in the f27 kernel.

@wrabcak  can you get someone from SELinux to look at this?

Sure, 
@Wonder93 @bachradsusi @vmojzis @zpytela PTAL.

In other words, pavel sosin can access  65354 "nobodies" because id's starting from 100000 belongs to the "nobody" diapazone. I'm not sure that "nobodies" are not restricted by something, like polkitt. It also assumes that user/group ids are long" and used as long in every API invocation. Users created by Anakonda have 16bits length ids. Long UID/GIDs may affect [User namespaces](https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man7/user_namespaces.7.html). Anakonda itself is not implemented perfectly in Fedora 35.

Yeah this will fix homectl users. Users created with --storage=fscrypt are still blocked by systemd-homed using v1 encryption policies but that's also being discussed upstream. Btrfs and subvolume based homes should start working if the uid mapping is fixed.
I'm testing the above patch [here](https://aur.archlinux.org/packages/systemd-homed-containers/) and it's working well with a btrfs subvol

> I'm testing the above patch [here](https://aur.archlinux.org/packages/systemd-homed-containers/) and it's working well with a btrfs subvol

Thanks for confirming it!

I think we can close this issue since there is nothing to do on the Podman side.
